global.title: Tesla still has no real plan for Hardware 3 owners promised FSD
global.summary: Elon Musk was asked directly on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call whether the company still plans to upgrade Hardware 3 (HW3) cars so they can run its latest “Full Self-Driving” software. global.title: Elon Musk hints at Tesla (TSLA)-SpaceX merger on earnings call
global.summary: Elon Musk hinted at a merger between Tesla and SpaceX on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call, pointing to “more and more overlap” between the two companies he controls. global.title: Tesla says ‘Robotaxi’ is expanding — its own chart shows it’s not
global.summary: Tesla told shareholders this week that its Robotaxi service is expanding. The company’s own chart proves it isn’t. global.title: Subaru is delaying the 3-row Getaway EV, following Toyota
global.summary: Subaru confirmed that production of the Getaway EV is being delayed, following Toyota’s decision to delay its first three-row electric SUV.

global.title: Tesla (TSLA) releases Q2 2026 financial results: record revenue, big profit miss
global.summary: Tesla (TSLA) released its financial results and shareholders’ letter for the second quarter (Q2) 2026 after market close today. global.title: Ford F-150 electric trucks can now power a home’s electrical panel
global.summary: Ford F-150 Lightning or hybrid owners can now use their trucks to keep hard-wired home essentials running during an outage by connecting their truck directly to their home’s main electrical panel. And even better, it’s affordable.

global.title: France won’t approve Tesla ‘FSD’ yet, wants EU-wide sign-off first
global.summary: France’s Transport Minister, Philippe Tabarot, says the country won’t authorize Tesla’s “Full Self-Driving” (Supervised) “as it currently stands,” pointing to two safety problems: the system lets drivers speed, and it doesn’t keep them attentive enough in cities. global.title: Toyota’s next-gen Corolla EV takes center stage as a ‘sense of crisis’ reshapes the company
global.summary: The world’s best-selling car of all time is going electric. Toyota’s next-generation Corolla EV will debut on a new platform as “a considerable sense of crisis” is forcing the company to reinvent itself.
